methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
OSH Cut is a bootstrapped robotics and manufacturing company founded by Caleb and Jacom Chamberlain. The valuation universe comprises nine total comparables: one publicly traded entity (277810.KQ) and eight private companies, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity. The sector peer set totals seven firms, though no meaningful cluster of same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables exists.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. Of the seven robotics and manufacturing peers identified, zero operate at OSH Cut's bootstrapped stage, zero sit in adjacent stages, and zero fall within a similar valuation tier. The filtering criteria require a minimum of three datapoints to support triangulation; the available set lacks sufficient homogeneity for reliable benchmarking. A sector-wide median would risk systematic bias by conflating early-stage bootstrapped operations with later-stage, venture-backed competitors carrying materially different risk and capital structures.

Given these constraints, traditional valuation triangulation cannot be completed with confidence. A defensible analysis would require either: expansion of the peer set to include non-sector bootstrapped companies with comparable revenue or growth profiles; direct revenue or unit economics data from OSH Cut; or reliance on cost-of-capital methods anchored to founder equity stakes and capital deployment. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of a sufficiently dense, stage-appropriate comparable universe in this sector.
